package repository

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Repository constructor accepts *gin.Engine or *gin.RouterGroup.
// This tightly couples the repository to the HTTP framework.

type Product struct {
	ID    string `gorm:"column:id"`
	Name  string `gorm:"column:name"`
	Price float64 `gorm:"column:price"`
}

type ProductRepository struct {
	db     *gorm.DB
	engine *gin.Engine
}

// NewProductRepository accepts *gin.Engine - this is the anti-pattern.
// A repository constructor should only accept persistence-related dependencies.
func NewProductRepository(db *gorm.DB, engine *gin.Engine) *ProductRepository {
	return &ProductRepository{
		db:     db,
		engine: engine,
	}
}

// NewProductRepositoryWithRouter accepts *gin.RouterGroup - same anti-pattern.
func NewProductRepositoryWithRouter(db *gorm.DB, router *gin.RouterGroup) *ProductRepository {
	repo := &ProductRepository{db: db}
	router.GET("/products", repo.handleList)
	router.POST("/products", repo.handleCreate)
	return repo
}

func (r *ProductRepository) handleList(c *gin.Context) {
	var products []Product
	r.db.Find(&products)
	c.JSON(200, products)
}

func (r *ProductRepository) handleCreate(c *gin.Context) {
	var product Product
	c.ShouldBindJSON(&product)
	r.db.Create(&product)
	c.JSON(201, product)
}

func (r *ProductRepository) FindByID(id string) (*Product, error) {
	var product Product
	err := r.db.First(&product, "id = ?", id).Error
	return &product, err
}
